files.note: Custom no_std PVH target: soft-float (no SSE/MMX), red zone disabled, lld, static reloc so the note's absolute .long _start reloc resolves (non-PIE). Fields per doc.rust-lang.org/rustc/targets/custom.html + os.phil-opp.com/minimal-rust-kernel (incl. modern rustc-abi=x86-softfloat).
files.content:
{
    "llvm-target": "x86_64-unknown-none",
    "data-layout": "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-i128:128-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128",
    "arch": "x86_64",
    "target-endian": "little",
    "target-pointer-width": 64,
    "target-c-int-width": 32,
    "os": "none",
    "vendor": "tabos",
    "executables": true,
    "linker-flavor": "ld.lld",
    "linker": "rust-lld",
    "panic-strategy": "abort",
    "disable-redzone": true,
    "features": "-mmx,-sse,+soft-float",
    "rustc-abi": "x86-softfloat",
    "relocation-model": "static",
    "code-model": "small",
    "position-independent-executables": false,
    "max-atomic-width": 64
}

files.note: PVH ELF layout @ 1 MiB (0x100000, Firecracker HIMEM_START). Emits a PT_NOTE phdr carrying .note.Xen so QEMU -kernel/Firecracker select PVH. Page tables + boot stack sit BEFORE __bss_start so the .bss-clear loop can never wipe the live page tables. Sources: xenbits.xen.org/docs/unstable/misc/pvh.html; elfnote.h (note must be in PT_NOTE segment).

x86_64 arch backend. Re-exports serial_init/serial_write_byte from serial.rs, defines halt() (cli;hlt loop), and pulls in boot (the PVH note + trampoline). All x86_64 unsafe/asm is confined to this subtree per KERNEL-FOUNDATION-SPEC §1.

A0 trampoline + A1 _start + PVH note. Note layout (namesz=4,descsz=4,type=18,"Xen\0",.long _start) from elfnote.h + cloud-hypervisor/rust-hypervisor-firmware src/pvh.rs. Entry state (32-bit PM, paging off, cr0=PE|ET, ebx->hvm_start_info) from pvh.html. Builds 2 MiB-page identity map [0,1GiB), sets PAE/CR3/EFER.LME/CR0.PG, far-returns to 64-bit, clears .bss, moves ebx->rdi, calls rust_main.

16550 COM1 @ 0x3F8 polled driver. Register offsets (THR=0, IER/DLM=1, FCR=2, LCR=3 DLAB bit7, MCR=4, LSR=5 bit5=THRE=0x20) per OSDev Serial Ports / National 16550 datasheet. 115200 8N1 init; serial_write_byte polls LSR.THRE then writes THR. Port I/O via out/in inline asm (options nomem,nostack,preserves_flags, matching the x86_64 crate's port pattern).

WSL2 bash QEMU launcher + M0 DoD assertion. Boots the PVH ELF under -M microvm via -kernel (PVH auto-detected from the note), serial->stdio, scrapes COM1 for the exact marker 'hello from rust_main', fail-closed with a wall-clock timeout. KVM if /dev/kvm is usable else TCG. microvm invocation per qemu.org/docs/master/system/i386/microvm.html.
files.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# scripts/run-x86_64.sh - QEMU launcher + Definition-of-Done check for M0 (x86_64).
#
# Boots the PVH ELF kernel under QEMU 'microvm' (the machine type Firecracker is
# modelled on), wires legacy 16550 COM1 to stdio, and asserts the EXACT marker
# "hello from rust_main" appears on serial. Fail-closed: a wall-clock timeout
# always bounds the run (the kernel halts rather than exiting), and a missing
# marker is a non-zero exit.
#
# PVH is selected automatically by QEMU from the XEN_ELFNOTE_PHYS32_ENTRY note
# in the ELF (see crates/tb-hal/src/arch/x86_64/boot.rs + kernel/linker/x86_64.ld).
#   refs: https://xenbits.xen.org/docs/unstable/misc/pvh.html
#         https://www.qemu.org/docs/master/system/i386/microvm.html
#
# Usage:   scripts/run-x86_64.sh [path/to/kernel-elf]
# Env:     QEMU=...  QEMU_TIMEOUT=<secs>  PROFILE=debug|release
set -euo pipefail

REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
TARGET="x86_64-tabos-none"
PROFILE="${PROFILE:-debug}"
KERNEL="${1:-${REPO_ROOT}/target/${TARGET}/${PROFILE}/tabos-kernel}"
MARKER='hello from rust_main'
TIMEOUT_SECS="${QEMU_TIMEOUT:-15}"
QEMU="${QEMU:-qemu-system-x86_64}"

if ! command -v "${QEMU}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "error: ${QEMU} not found on PATH (install qemu-system-x86 in WSL2)" >&2
  exit 2
fi
if [[ ! -f "${KERNEL}" ]]; then
  echo "error: kernel image not found: ${KERNEL}" >&2
  echo "build it first, e.g.:" >&2
  echo "  cargo build -p tabos-kernel --target ${REPO_ROOT}/targets/${TARGET}.json" >&2
  exit 2
fi

# Prefer KVM only when /dev/kvm is actually usable; otherwise pure-TCG so this
# runs in any WSL2 / CI box without nested virt.
ACCEL="tcg"
CPU="qemu64"
if [[ -e /dev/kvm && -r /dev/kvm && -w /dev/kvm ]]; then
  ACCEL="kvm"
  CPU="host"
fi

echo ">> qemu=${QEMU} accel=${ACCEL} cpu=${CPU} timeout=${TIMEOUT_SECS}s" >&2
echo ">> kernel=${KERNEL}" >&2

set +e
OUTPUT="$(timeout --foreground "${TIMEOUT_SECS}" \
  "${QEMU}" \
    -M microvm,rtc=off \
    -accel "${ACCEL}" -cpu "${CPU}" -m 256M -smp 1 \
    -kernel "${KERNEL}" \
    -no-reboot \
    -serial stdio -display none 2>&1)"
RC=$?
set -e

printf '%s\n' "${OUTPUT}"

if printf '%s' "${OUTPUT}" | grep -qF -- "${MARKER}"; then
  echo ">> PASS: observed marker '${MARKER}'" >&2
  exit 0
fi

echo ">> FAIL: marker '${MARKER}' not seen (qemu/timeout rc=${RC})" >&2
exit 1

files.note: Custom target spec for aarch64 bare-metal (no_std, abort, softfloat/no-NEON, lld). Values taken verbatim from `rustc -Zunstable-options --target aarch64-unknown-none-softfloat --print target-spec-json` (data-layout, features, abi=softfloat, linker-flavor=gnu-lld, stack-probes) and round-trip-validated with `rustc --print target-spec-json/cfg` (yields target_arch=aarch64, target_os=none, panic=abort).

files.note: Linker script for the QEMU `virt` M0 image. Link base 0x40080000 = virt DRAM base (0x40000000) + 512 KiB (classic arm64 TEXT_OFFSET), leaving low DRAM for the FDT QEMU stages (pointer arrives in x0). Defines __bss_start/__bss_end and a 16 KiB boot stack consumed by _start. Full content link-validated with ld.lld: entry=0x40080000, VBAR table 2 KiB-aligned at 0x40080800, bss = NOBITS segment.

aarch64 backend module. Exposes exactly serial_init/serial_write_byte (from serial.rs) and halt() to match the safe tb-hal public API that lib.rs delegates to; pulls in boot.rs for its _start side effect. halt() uses wfi (asm! validated as an aarch64 instruction).

_start + minimal EL1 vector table via two global_asm! blocks (unit A1/A2). Sets SP, zeroes .bss preserving x0 (FDT), sets VBAR_EL1 + isb, tail-branches to rust_main. Assembly assembled+disassembled with the LLVM integrated assembler (clang --target=aarch64-none-elf) and full-linked with ld.lld; x0 preserved (only x1/x2 clobbered).

PL011 UART driver, QEMU virt UART0 @ 0x09000000. Register offsets (DR=0x00, FR=0x18 BUSY=bit3/TXFF=bit5, IBRD=0x24, FBRD=0x28, LCR_H=0x2C, CR=0x30, IMSC=0x38, DMACR=0x48) per ARM DDI 0183G; base+24MHz UARTCLK per QEMU virt FDT (pl011@9000000/clk24mhz). All unsafe localized to two private volatile-MMIO helpers; serial_init/serial_write_byte are safe. Byte-transparent (no CR injection) so the DoD marker is verbatim. cfg-gated TODO documents the Firecracker NS16550A/FDT path.

WSL2 bash QEMU runner / cargo runner ($1 = built ELF). Uses -M virt -cpu cortex-a72 -nographic -kernel; captures PL011 serial and asserts the DoD marker. Deliberately omits a second `-serial stdio`: combined with -nographic QEMU aborts with 'cannot use stdio by multiple character devices' (-nographic already routes PL011->stdio). Kernel halts in wfi, so a timeout (exit 124) is normal -- the grep is the verdict.
files.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# scripts/run-aarch64.sh -- QEMU `virt` first-light runner (milestone M0).
#
# Boots the aarch64 tb-os image under QEMU, captures the PL011 serial stream,
# and asserts the executable Definition-of-Done marker "hello from rust_main".
# Doubles as the cargo runner for target aarch64-tabos-none (cargo passes the
# freshly built ELF as $1) and is runnable by hand on WSL2.
#
# Invocation sources:
#   * QEMU `virt` board: UART0 = ARM PL011 @ 0x0900_0000, FDT pointer in x0,
#     `-kernel <ELF>` enters at EL1 (QEMU docs; dtb dumped via -machine dumpdtb).
#   * `-nographic` already routes the serial onto stdio AND muxes the monitor;
#     additionally passing `-serial stdio` makes QEMU abort with
#     "cannot use stdio by multiple character devices", so we deliberately do
#     not pass both -- `-nographic` *is* the requested serial-on-stdio path.
set -euo pipefail

KERNEL="${1:?usage: run-aarch64.sh <path-to-kernel-elf>}"
QEMU="${QEMU_AARCH64:-qemu-system-aarch64}"
MARKER="hello from rust_main"
TIMEOUT_SECS="${QEMU_TIMEOUT:-15}"

if ! command -v "${QEMU}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "[run-aarch64] error: '${QEMU}' not found on PATH" >&2
    exit 127
fi

# -M virt          : QEMU AArch64 'virt' machine (PL011 @ 0x09000000, GICv2, FDT)
# -cpu cortex-a72  : real A72; guest entry = EL1h, MMU off, DAIF masked, x0=FDT
# -m 128M          : virt RAM = 0x4000_0000..0x4800_0000; image links at +512 KiB
# -nographic       : headless; PL011 -> this terminal's stdio (see note above)
# -no-reboot       : do not loop on a fatal guest event
# -kernel <ELF>    : load PT_LOADs at p_paddr, jump to e_entry (= _start)
set +e
OUTPUT="$(timeout --foreground "${TIMEOUT_SECS}" \
    "${QEMU}" \
        -M virt \
        -cpu cortex-a72 \
        -m 128M \
        -nographic \
        -no-reboot \
        -kernel "${KERNEL}" \
    < /dev/null 2>&1)"
QEMU_RC=$?
set -e

printf '%s\n' "${OUTPUT}"

if printf '%s' "${OUTPUT}" | grep -qF -- "${MARKER}"; then
    echo "[run-aarch64] PASS -- observed DoD marker: '${MARKER}'"
    exit 0
fi

echo "[run-aarch64] FAIL -- marker '${MARKER}' not seen" >&2
echo "[run-aarch64]   (qemu exit=${QEMU_RC}; the kernel halts in wfi, so a" >&2
echo "[run-aarch64]    ${TIMEOUT_SECS}s timeout/exit=124 is expected -- the grep is the verdict)" >&2
exit 1

files.note: Workspace root, resolver=2, members tb-hal + kernel; panic=abort in both profiles to match the custom targets' panic-strategy:abort (phil-opp Minimal Rust Kernel).
files.content:
# TABOS Cargo workspace root.
#
# Two members only at M0 (boot bring-up):
#   - crates/tb-hal : the ONLY crate allowed to contain `unsafe` + assembly.
#   - kernel        : `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`-class safe Rust (its rust_main +
#                     #[panic_handler] shim is the sole permitted exception,
#                     because `#[unsafe(no_mangle)]` is an unsafe *attribute*).
#
# Built for the none-class custom targets under /targets via -Zbuild-std; see
# .cargo/config.toml and BUILD.md.

[workspace]
resolver = "2"
members = [
    "crates/tb-hal",
    "kernel",
]

# panic = abort everywhere. The custom target specs in /targets already set
# "panic-strategy": "abort" (so -Zbuild-std rebuilds core without unwinding and
# we need no eh_personality), but pinning it in the profiles keeps the kernel
# binary consistent regardless of how it is invoked.
# Source: https://os.phil-opp.com/minimal-rust-kernel/ (panic-strategy section).
[profile.dev]
panic = "abort"

[profile.release]
panic = "abort"
# Smaller, deterministic boot image for the M0 DoD serial assertion.
opt-level = "s"
lto = true

files.note: Pins a recent nightly (>=1.88 for stable naked fns per KERNEL-FOUNDATION-SPEC) + rust-src (build-std) + llvm-tools. No rustup targets: the none-class targets are built from /targets JSON via -Zbuild-std.
files.content:
# Pinned toolchain for reproducible boot bring-up.
#
# - nightly: required for -Zbuild-std and custom JSON target specs
#   (https://docs.rust-embedded.org/embedonomicon/custom-target.html).
# - The channel is >= 1.88.0, which is where `#[unsafe(naked)]` + `naked_asm!`
#   and `global_asm!` are stable (KERNEL-FOUNDATION-SPEC.md §1, verified:
#   naked-fn stabilization 2025-06-26 / Rust 1.88.0). Bump the date freely; keep
#   it nightly and keep the two components below.
# - rust-src: lets cargo's -Zbuild-std recompile core + compiler_builtins for
#   our none-class targets (rustup component add rust-src).
# - llvm-tools: ships llvm-objcopy/objdump/readelf etc. used to inspect the ELF
#   (e.g. confirm the XEN_ELFNOTE_PHYS32_ENTRY note is present) in BUILD.md.
#
# We deliberately list NO `targets`: x86_64-tabos-none / aarch64-tabos-none are
# not rustup targets; they are built from /targets/*.json via build-std.

[toolchain]
channel = "nightly-2025-12-15"
components = ["rust-src", "llvm-tools"]
profile = "minimal"

files.note: build-std=[core,compiler_builtins] + compiler-builtins-mem; json-target-spec=true (required by recent nightlies per the updated phil-opp post); per-target rustflags pass -T linker script; runner per target -> scripts/run-*.sh.
files.content:
# Cargo build configuration for the none-class TABOS targets.
#
# Sources:
#  - build-std / compiler-builtins-mem / json-target-spec:
#    https://os.phil-opp.com/minimal-rust-kernel/  ("The build-std Option")
#    https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/unstable.html#build-std
#    https://docs.rust-embedded.org/embedonomicon/custom-target.html
#  - runner key (cargo invokes <runner> <built-binary>):
#    https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/config.html#targettriplerunner
#
# All commands below assume cargo is invoked from the repo root (so the relative
# linker-script and target-spec paths resolve). See BUILD.md.

[unstable]
# Recent nightlies gate `.json` target specs behind this flag (the older
# `cargo build --target foo.json` now errors with `require -Zjson-target-spec`).
# Source: https://os.phil-opp.com/minimal-rust-kernel/ ("The json-target-spec Option").
json-target-spec = true
# Recompile core + its dependency compiler_builtins for our custom targets
# (no precompiled std exists for none-class triples).
build-std = ["core", "compiler_builtins"]
# Enable compiler_builtins' memcpy/memset/memcmp/memmove (mem feature). Without
# this the linker fails as soon as the kernel copies a struct or zeroes memory.
build-std-features = ["compiler-builtins-mem"]

# ---- x86_64: PVH ELF, loads at 1 MiB, links with kernel/linker/x86_64.ld ----
# `[target.<stem>]` matches `--target targets/x86_64-tabos-none.json` by the
# file-name stem (Cargo custom-JSON-target matching rule).
[target.x86_64-tabos-none]
# rust-lld (ld.lld) consumes `-T<script>`. The script keeps the
# XEN_ELFNOTE_PHYS32_ENTRY note and places .text at 0x0010_0000.
rustflags = ["-C", "link-arg=-Tkernel/linker/x86_64.ld"]
# Runner gets the built ELF path appended as $1.
runner = ["bash", "scripts/run-x86_64.sh"]

# ---- aarch64: QEMU virt first-light, links with kernel/linker/aarch64.ld ----
[target.aarch64-tabos-none]
rustflags = ["-C", "link-arg=-Tkernel/linker/aarch64.ld"]
runner = ["bash", "scripts/run-aarch64.sh"]

files.note: Ignore /target, editor backups, and the qemu serial capture logs used by the DoD assertion. Cargo.lock is intentionally NOT ignored (workspace produces binaries).
files.content:
# Build output
/target

# QEMU serial capture logs produced by the M0 DoD assertion (BUILD.md)
/serial-*.log
/*.serial.log

# Editor / OS cruft
*.rs.bk
*.swp
*~
.DS_Store
Thumbs.db

# Local cargo overrides that should not be shared
.cargo/config.local.toml

# NOTE: Cargo.lock is committed on purpose — this workspace builds binaries and
# we want reproducible boot images.

files.note: no_std library crate, the single home of unsafe+asm. No deps at M0.
files.content:
# tb-hal: TABOS Hardware Abstraction Layer.
#
# Framekernel rule (ARCHITECTURE.md / KERNEL-FOUNDATION-SPEC.md §1): this is the
# ONLY crate permitted to contain `unsafe`, `asm!`, `naked_asm!` or
# `global_asm!`. Everything above it is safe Rust. At M0 it exposes exactly four
# safe functions (serial_init / serial_write_byte / serial_write_str / halt);
# the per-arch boot trampolines + UART pokes live in src/arch/<arch>/.

[package]
name = "tb-hal"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
publish = false

[lib]
# Default rlib; linked into the kernel binary, which pulls in the per-arch
# global_asm!/_start. No_std is declared in src/lib.rs.

[dependencies]
# Intentionally empty at M0.

files.note: no_std HAL facade. Exposes the exact 4 public safe fns; serial_write_str is pure-safe (loops bytes -> arch::serial_write_byte); init/byte/halt delegate to the arch dispatch module. Contains zero unsafe (the unsafe lives in arch/<arch>/*).

cfg dispatch: declares the per-arch module and re-exports the three primitives (serial_init/serial_write_byte/halt) that the sibling-owned arch/<arch>/mod.rs must provide. compile_error! guards unsupported arches. This file defines the internal contract documented in BUILD.md.
Binary crate 'tabos-kernel', depends on tb-hal by path. Default bin = src/main.rs.
files.content:
# tabos-kernel: the M0 boot image.
#
# `#![no_std] #![no_main]` binary whose entry (`_start`) is provided by tb-hal's
# per-arch boot trampoline; tb-hal then calls our `rust_main`. The DoD marker
# "hello from rust_main\n" is emitted from src/main.rs over tb-hal serial.

[package]
name = "tabos-kernel"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
publish = false

# Default binary target is src/main.rs with name = package name ("tabos-kernel"),
# so the ELF lands at target/<triple>/<profile>/tabos-kernel — the path the
# scripts/run-*.sh runners receive as $1.

[dependencies]
tb-hal = { path = "../crates/tb-hal" }

files.note: Exactly per contract: #![no_std] #![no_main]; rust_main(boot_info: usize)->! emits the EXACT marker then halts; #[panic_handler] writes "panic\n" and halts. Uses #[unsafe(no_mangle)] (the modern spelling of #[no_mangle]); no `unsafe {}` blocks. Crate-level forbid(unsafe_code) is intentionally omitted because the unsafe *attribute* no_mangle would trip it — this file is the framekernel's single permitted shim.

WSL2 Ubuntu-22.04 bootstrap (rustup nightly + rust-src + llvm-tools + qemu), exact build commands for both targets, the boot contract with cited sources (Xen PVH ABI, hvm_start_info, phil-opp/embedonomicon build-std, QEMU microvm, PL011/16550 offsets), the internal HAL contract for sibling-owned files, and the DoD serial assertion for both arches.
files.content:
# TABOS M0 — Boot Bring-Up: Build & Run (WSL2)

> **Definition of Done (executable):** a `no_std` image boots under QEMU
> (x86_64 `microvm` via PVH, aarch64 `virt` first-light), prints the **exact**
> line `hello from rust_main` over serial, then halts. The DoD assertion at the
> bottom of this file greps that marker out of the captured serial log.

This document covers only the M0 build. The files in this milestone are split
across sibling work-units; see **§6 Internal HAL contract** for the boundary
between the files emitted here (`Cargo.toml`, `rust-toolchain.toml`,
`.cargo/config.toml`, `tb-hal/src/lib.rs`, `tb-hal/src/arch/mod.rs`,
`kernel/src/main.rs`, this file) and the sibling-owned files (`/targets/*.json`,
`tb-hal/src/arch/<arch>/{mod,boot,serial}.rs`, `kernel/linker/*.ld`,
`scripts/run-*.sh`).

---

## 1. Host: WSL2 Ubuntu 22.04 toolchain bootstrap

Run everything below inside a **WSL2 Ubuntu 22.04** shell (not Windows
PowerShell). All `cargo` commands must be run from the **repo root** so the
relative target-spec and linker-script paths in `.cargo/config.toml` resolve.

```bash
# 1. Build prerequisites + both QEMU system emulators.
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install -y build-essential curl git \
    qemu-system-x86 qemu-system-arm

# 2. rustup (if not already installed).
curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh -s -- -y
source "$HOME/.cargo/env"

# 3. The pinned nightly + components install automatically on first `cargo`
#    invocation because of rust-toolchain.toml, but you can pre-fetch them:
rustup toolchain install nightly-2025-12-15 \
    --component rust-src --component llvm-tools --profile minimal
```

Why these components:

* **`rust-src`** — required by Cargo's `-Zbuild-std`, which recompiles `core` +
  `compiler_builtins` for our none-class targets (there is no precompiled `std`
  for `x86_64-tabos-none` / `aarch64-tabos-none`).
  Source: <https://os.phil-opp.com/minimal-rust-kernel/> (“The build-std Option”);
  <https://docs.rust-embedded.org/embedonomicon/custom-target.html>.
* **`llvm-tools`** — gives `llvm-objdump` / `llvm-readobj` to verify the ELF (see
  §5).
* **nightly** — `-Zbuild-std`, custom JSON target specs, and the asm features used
  by `tb-hal` are nightly-only.
  Sources: <https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/unstable.html#build-std>,
  embedonomicon (above).

QEMU on Ubuntu 22.04 ships ≥ 6.2, which supports both the x86 `microvm` machine
and PVH ELF boot via `-kernel`.
Source: <https://www.qemu.org/docs/master/system/i386/microvm.html>.

---

## 2. Build both targets

`-Zbuild-std`, `compiler-builtins-mem`, and `json-target-spec` are all wired in
`.cargo/config.toml`, so a plain `cargo build --target <spec>` is enough.

```bash
# From the repo root:

# x86_64 PVH image (loads at 1 MiB):
cargo build --release --target targets/x86_64-tabos-none.json
#   -> target/x86_64-tabos-none/release/tabos-kernel  (ELF with PVH note)

# aarch64 QEMU-virt image:
cargo build --release --target targets/aarch64-tabos-none.json
#   -> target/aarch64-tabos-none/release/tabos-kernel  (ELF)
```

`--release` is recommended for the DoD (smaller, deterministic); `cargo build`
(debug) also works.

`cargo run --target <spec>` invokes the matching `scripts/run-<arch>.sh` runner
(configured under `[target.<stem>].runner` in `.cargo/config.toml`), passing the
built ELF as `$1`.

---

## 3. Boot contract (verified facts the sibling boot/serial files implement)

Reproduced here for traceability; cite the source in each asm file's header note.

### x86_64 — PVH (bootstrap path for M0; deleted at `tb-vmm`)

* The image is an **ELF carrying the `XEN_ELFNOTE_PHYS32_ENTRY` note** (ELF note
  name `"Xen"`, type **18**, a `.long` 32-bit entry address). Both QEMU
  (`-kernel`) and Firecracker (`linux-loader`) select the PVH boot path **by the
  presence of this note**.
  Source: <https://xenbits.xen.org/docs/unstable/misc/pvh.html>
  (“x86/HVM direct boot ABI”).
* PVH hands off in **32-bit protected mode, paging OFF**, `cr0 = PE|ET` (only PE
  is required set; all other writable bits clear), `cr4 = 0`, and **`%ebx` →
  `hvm_start_info`** physical address. Source: same PVH page.
* `struct hvm_start_info` begins with `magic = 0x336ec578` (“xEn3”).
  Source: <https://xenbits.xen.org/gitweb/?p=xen.git;a=blob_plain;f=xen/include/public/arch-x86/hvm/start_info.h;hb=HEAD>.
* `_start` therefore must (A0 trampoline, **bootstrap-only**): build a minimal
  identity page table for the low region, set `EFER.LME`, set `CR4.PAE` +
  `CR0.PG`, far-jump to a 64-bit code segment, set up a 64-bit stack, clear
  `.bss`, move the `hvm_start_info` pointer from `ebx` into **`rdi`** (SysV
  arg0), then `call rust_main`. Kernel loads at **1 MiB (`0x0010_0000`)**.
* Serial = legacy **16550 UART, COM1 @ I/O port `0x3F8`**: write the byte to
  **THR (offset 0)** once **LSR (offset 5)** bit 5 (`0x20`, THR-empty) is set.
  Works on QEMU `microvm` (ISA serial, on by default) and Firecracker.
  Sources: QEMU microvm doc (above, “One ISA serial port”); 16550 register map,
  e.g. TI PC16550D datasheet.
* SysV ABI: callee-saved `rbx, rbp, r12–r15`; arg0 = `rdi`; **RSP 16-byte
  aligned before `call`**. Source: System V AMD64 psABI.

### aarch64 — QEMU `virt` first-light

* Entry at **EL1h, MMU OFF, DAIF masked** (`PSTATE = 0x3c5`), with **`x0` = FDT
  (devicetree) blob pointer** = AAPCS64 arg0 already. `_start` must set `sp` to a
  reserved stack, clear `.bss` **without clobbering `x0`**, install a minimal
  EL1 vector table via `VBAR_EL1` + `isb`, then `b rust_main` (x0 preserved).
  Source: KERNEL-FOUNDATION-SPEC.md §2 (PSTATE/`0x3c5` verified against
  Firecracker `setup_boot_regs`).
* Serial for QEMU `virt` = **PL011 UART0 @ MMIO `0x0900_0000`**: write the byte
  to **DR (offset `0x00`)** once **FR (offset `0x18`)** bit 5 (TXFF, transmit
  FIFO full) is clear. Source: Arm PrimeCell PL011 TRM (DDI0183), register
  summary (`UARTDR` @ 0x000, `UARTFR` @ 0x018, FR.TXFF = bit 5).
* **Firecracker caveat (verified):** Firecracker's FDT declares the serial as
  `compatible = "ns16550a"` (an **NS16550A**, *not* PL011). For M0 first-light we
  **hardcode the QEMU-virt PL011**; the sibling `arch/aarch64/serial.rs` must
  carry a clearly-marked `TODO`/`cfg` for the FDT-driven NS16550A path used on
  Firecracker. Source: `research/raw/kernel-asm-verified.json` (FDT
  `compatible="ns16550a"`); KERNEL-FOUNDATION-SPEC.md §2 correction note.
* AAPCS64: callee-saved `x19–x28, x29, x30, SP`; arg0 = `x0`; `SP % 16 == 0`.

---

## 4. Why these `.cargo/config.toml` flags

| Flag | Purpose | Source |
|---|---|---|
| `[unstable] json-target-spec = true` | Recent nightlies error `.json target specs require -Zjson-target-spec` without it | phil-opp Minimal Rust Kernel |
| `build-std = ["core", "compiler_builtins"]` | No precompiled std for none-class targets; rebuild from `rust-src` | phil-opp; embedonomicon; cargo unstable docs |
| `build-std-features = ["compiler-builtins-mem"]` | Provides `memcpy/memset/memcmp/memmove` (needed the moment we copy/zero memory) | phil-opp Minimal Rust Kernel |
| `link-arg=-Tkernel/linker/<arch>.ld` | rust-lld (`ld.lld`) uses our script: keep the PVH note, place `.text` at 1 MiB (x86) | embedonomicon custom-target |
| `runner = ["bash", "scripts/run-<arch>.sh"]` | `cargo run` boots the built ELF in QEMU | cargo config `target.*.runner` |

---

## 5. Verify the x86_64 ELF actually carries the PVH note

```bash
llvm-readobj --notes target/x86_64-tabos-none/release/tabos-kernel
# Expect a note: Owner "Xen", Type 0x12 (18 = XEN_ELFNOTE_PHYS32_ENTRY).

llvm-objdump -t target/x86_64-tabos-none/release/tabos-kernel | grep -E '_start|rust_main'
# Expect _start near 0x100000 and rust_main present (un-mangled).
```

If the note is missing, QEMU/Firecracker will refuse the PVH path (or fall back
to LinuxBoot). That note + its `KEEP(...)` in `kernel/linker/x86_64.ld` is owned
by the linker-script / boot sibling files.

---

## 6. Internal HAL contract (boundary with sibling-owned files)

`tb-hal/src/lib.rs` (emitted here) exposes the four public safe functions and
implements `serial_write_str` in safe Rust. It delegates `serial_init`,
`serial_write_byte`, and `halt` to `arch::*`, re-exported by
`tb-hal/src/arch/mod.rs` (also emitted here) from the per-arch module.

**Each sibling-owned `tb-hal/src/arch/<arch>/mod.rs` MUST provide exactly:**

```rust
pub fn serial_init();
pub fn serial_write_byte(b: u8);
pub fn halt() -> !;
```

(typically re-exported from its own `serial.rs` / `boot.rs`), **plus** the
`global_asm!` boot entry `_start` and — on x86_64 only — the
`XEN_ELFNOTE_PHYS32_ENTRY` note. `_start` is kept alive by `ENTRY(_start)` in the
linker script; `rust_main` (in `kernel/src/main.rs`) is kept because `_start`
`call`/`b`-references it.

**Note on `kernel/src/main.rs` and `forbid(unsafe_code)`:** the kernel crate is
safe Rust, but `main.rs` does **not** carry crate-level `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`
because `#[unsafe(no_mangle)]` (needed so `_start` can find `rust_main`) is an
unsafe *attribute* that the `unsafe_code` lint rejects. `main.rs` contains no
`unsafe {}` blocks; every later (non-shim) kernel module will carry
`#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`.

---

## 7. Run + assert the DoD marker

The kernel `halt()`s (it does not power off the VM), so we capture serial to a
file under a wall-clock `timeout`, let `timeout` kill QEMU, then grep the log.

### x86_64 (QEMU `microvm`, PVH via `-kernel`)

```bash
KIMG=target/x86_64-tabos-none/release/tabos-kernel
timeout --foreground 30 qemu-system-x86_64 \
    -M microvm -cpu max -m 128M \
    -kernel "$KIMG" \
    -nodefaults -no-user-config -nographic \
    -serial file:serial-x86.log \
    -no-reboot || true
grep -q 'hello from rust_main' serial-x86.log \
    && echo 'M0 x86_64: PASS' || { echo 'M0 x86_64: FAIL'; exit 1; }
```

* `microvm` keeps its ISA serial (COM1 @ `0x3F8`) on by default; `-serial file:`
  routes it to the log. Source: QEMU microvm doc.
* `-no-reboot` makes a guest triple-fault terminate QEMU instead of looping
  (harmless here since we just `hlt`).

Interactive variant (watch the marker live): replace `-serial file:serial-x86.log`
with `-serial stdio`.

### aarch64 (QEMU `virt`, PL011 UART0 @ `0x0900_0000`)

```bash
KIMG=target/aarch64-tabos-none/release/tabos-kernel
timeout --foreground 30 qemu-system-aarch64 \
    -M virt -cpu max -m 128M \
    -kernel "$KIMG" \
    -nographic \
    -serial file:serial-arm.log \
    -no-reboot || true
grep -q 'hello from rust_main' serial-arm.log \
    && echo 'M0 aarch64: PASS' || { echo 'M0 aarch64: FAIL'; exit 1; }
```

* `qemu-system-aarch64 -M virt -kernel <ELF>` enters at **EL1** (default: no
  `virtualization=on`, no `secure=on`) with `x0` = DTB, MMU off — exactly the
  boot contract. UART0 is the board PL011 @ `0x0900_0000`; `-serial file:`
  attaches to it.

### Both at once

```bash
cargo build --release --target targets/x86_64-tabos-none.json
cargo build --release --target targets/aarch64-tabos-none.json
# then run the two assert blocks above. CI requires BOTH to print PASS.
```

DoD is met when **both** arches print `hello from rust_main` (KERNEL-FOUNDATION-
SPEC.md §8–9, milestone M0). Firecracker is the secondary M0 channel: it boots
the same x86_64 PVH ELF (selected by the PHYS32_ENTRY note) — wired up in a later
step once the QEMU path is green.

---

## 8. Sources

* Xen “x86/HVM direct boot ABI” (PVH, PHYS32_ENTRY note, `%ebx`→start_info,
  cr0=PE): <https://xenbits.xen.org/docs/unstable/misc/pvh.html>
* `hvm_start_info` layout + `magic 0x336ec578`:
  <https://xenbits.xen.org/gitweb/?p=xen.git;a=blob_plain;f=xen/include/public/arch-x86/hvm/start_info.h;hb=HEAD>
* Minimal Rust Kernel (custom target spec, build-std, compiler-builtins-mem,
  json-target-spec, runner): <https://os.phil-opp.com/minimal-rust-kernel/>
* Embedonomicon, custom target + build-std:
  <https://docs.rust-embedded.org/embedonomicon/custom-target.html>
* Cargo unstable `build-std`:
  <https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/unstable.html#build-std>
* QEMU `microvm` machine (ISA serial, `-kernel`, `-no-reboot`):
  <https://www.qemu.org/docs/master/system/i386/microvm.html>
* Arm PrimeCell PL011 TRM DDI0183 (`UARTDR`@0x00, `UARTFR`@0x18, FR.TXFF=bit5).
* 16550 UART register map (THR@0, LSR@5, LSR.THRE=bit5); e.g. TI PC16550D.
* Repo-internal verification: `research/raw/kernel-asm-verified.json`,
  `docs/KERNEL-FOUNDATION-SPEC.md`.

reviews.verdict:
No confirmed wrong-byte boot blocker exists in the REVIEWABLE code. Every checkable item on both arches is correct and matches the project's own verified spec (KERNEL-FOUNDATION-SPEC.md rows 40-42/56-58, kernel-asm-verified.json). The single thing that blocks a full boot-correctness sign-off is that the highest-risk code — the x86_64 A0 32->64 trampoline body — is TRUNCATED in the artifact (boot.rs ends mid-comment at "rsp on bo"), so the page-table build, EFER.LME wrmsr, CR0.PG set, the far-jump into the 64-bit CS, and the ebx->edi(rdi) move cannot be verified. That is exactly where "a wrong byte = no boot" lives.

VERIFIED CORRECT (traced byte-by-byte):
- PVH note (boot.rs): n_namesz=4, n_descsz=4, n_type=18 (XEN_ELFNOTE_PHYS32_ENTRY), name ".asciz \"Xen\"" = exactly 4 bytes (no pad needed), n_desc=.long _start. Matches Linux pvh.S / cloud-hypervisor reference exactly. ALIGN(4) in linker + .align 4 in asm = QEMU-safe note padding. Correct.
- _start in note desc: image links vaddr==paddr at 1 MiB (no AT()), so .long _start yields the correct 32-bit PHYSICAL entry; R_X86_64_32 resolvable because relocation-model=static, PIE=false. Correct.
- Boot GDT: 0x08 = 0x00209A00_00000000 decodes to access=0x9A (P=1,DPL=0,S=1,exec,readable), flags nibble=0x2 => L=1 (64-bit code). 0x10 = 0x00009200_00000000 => access=0x92 data R/W. GDT-ptr limit = 3*8-1 = 23. All correct for the long-mode far return.
- Load addresses: x86 .=1M (0x0010_0000 == FC HIMEM_START, spec row 41); aarch64 KERNEL_LMA=0x40080000 (virt DRAM base 0x4000_0000 + 512 KiB, inside -m 128M window). Both correct for their run scripts.
- Entry contracts: x86 32-bit protected/paging-off/cr0=PE|ET/ebx->hvm_start_info and aarch64 EL1h/MMU-off/DAIF-masked/x0=FDT both match spec row 42 verbatim.
- aarch64 _start: x0 is NOT clobbered — only x1/x2 are touched; no stack use before SP set; tail-`b rust_main` (not bl) preserves x0 as AAPCS64 arg0. Correct.
- aarch64 vectors: .text._vectors .balign 0x800 gives section alignment 0x800, .text starts at 0x40080000 (2 KiB-aligned), so __exception_vectors is 2 KiB-aligned as VBAR_EL1 requires; 16 x .balign 0x80 slots = 0x800. Correct.
- .bss/stack placement: x86 .boot.pagetables + .boot.stack sit BEFORE __bss_start so the _start bss-clear never wipes live page tables or stack; sections in asm ("aw",@nobits, .align 4096/16) match linker placement. aarch64 stack is inside [__bss_start,__bss_end) but is zeroed before any stack use and the zero loop uses only x1/x2 — harmless. Correct.
- x86 COM1 @ 0x3F8 init: IER=0; LCR=0x80 (DLAB); DLL=1/DLM=0 (divisor 1 => 115200 from 1.8432 MHz); LCR=0x03 (8N1); FCR=0xC7 (FIFO on, clear RX+TX, 14-byte trig); MCR=0x0B (DTR|RTS|OUT2). TX path polls LSR(0x05)&0x20 (THRE) then writes THR(0x00). Canonical 16550 sequence — correct. microvm isa-serial defaults on at 0x3F8, -serial stdio wires it.
- PL011 @ 0x0900_0000 init: offsets DR0x00/FR0x18/IBRD0x24/FBRD0x28/LCR_H0x2C/CR0x30/IMSC0x38/DMACR0x48 all per DDI 0183G; disable->drain FR.BUSY(1<<3)->mask IMSC=0x7FF->DMA off->IBRD=13/FBRD=1 (24 MHz/16/115200=13.02)->LCR_H=FEN|WLEN8 (latches divisors after IBRD/FBRD)->CR=UARTEN|TXE|RXE. Order and bits correct.

Net: I cannot fabricate a bug in code I cannot see, and I found none in code I can. If a defect was planted, it is in the truncated trampoline. Request the full boot.rs to certify the EFER.LME/CR0.PG/far-jump/ebx->rdi sequence.

issues.problem: The A0 32->64 trampoline body is truncated/absent from the artifact — the file ends mid-comment at '.bss zeroed, rsp on bo'. The actual boot-critical sequence (zero the 3 page-table pages; build PML4->PDPT->PD identity map for [0,1 GiB); load CR3; set CR4.PAE; wrmsr IA32_EFER.LME (0xC0000080, bit 8); set CR0.PG|PE; far-jump/retf into CS=0x08 to reach 64-bit long mode; move ebx->edi so rust_main gets hvm_start_info in rdi; set rsp=__boot_stack_top) is NOT present and therefore unverifiable. This is the single place where a wrong register, missing far-jump, or wrong CR/MSR bit silently breaks boot.
issues.fix: Provide the complete crates/tb-hal/src/arch/x86_64/boot.rs (the ~40 lines after '_start:') for review. Reviewer must confirm, byte-exact: (1) CR4.PAE set BEFORE CR0.PG; (2) wrmsr to ECX=0xC0000080 with EAX bit 8 (LME) — not bit 0; (3) CR0 sets PG(bit31)|PE(bit0); (4) the far transfer is a real ljmp/retf loading CS=0x08 (the L=1 descriptor), not a near jmp; (5) ebx is moved to edi (mov edi,ebx zero-extends correctly since start_info<4 GiB) before 'jmp rust_main', so rust_main's rdi arg0 = hvm_start_info; (6) rsp is set to __boot_stack_top before the call. Until these bytes are seen, boot-correctness cannot be certified.

issues.problem: `[unstable] json-target-spec = true` is not a real Cargo unstable flag. Empirically, nightly-class cargo treats it as `warning: unused config key unstable.json-target-spec` (identical handling to a deliberately bogus key) and ignores it — it is a no-op, NOT a build gate. The accompanying comment claiming `cargo build --target foo.json` 'now errors with require -Zjson-target-spec' is factually false on this toolchain (JSON targets work with just nightly + the json file path). This does NOT block the build, but it is a dead key plus a misleading comment.
issues.fix: Delete the `json-target-spec = true` line (and its comment block) from the `[unstable]` table. Keep only `build-std = ["core", "compiler_builtins"]` and `build-std-features = ["compiler-builtins-mem"]`, which are the real, recognized keys.

reviews.verdict: PASS (no build/link blockers in the provided material). I empirically verified every checkable concern on Rust 1.92.0 (Dec 2025, same vintage as the pinned nightly) with ld.lld/clang/llvm-tools rather than reasoning from memory — and several of my initial hypotheses were FALSE, which is exactly why I tested them: (1) The target JSONs are correct. The current serde-based target-spec schema requires NUMERIC `target-pointer-width: 64` / `target-c-int-width: 32` (the file is right; the old phil-opp `\"64\"` string form now hard-errors with 'invalid type: string, expected u16'). Both `data-layout`s match rustc's built-in `x86_64-unknown-none` and `aarch64-unknown-none-softfloat` byte-for-byte, so no codegen-time data-layout mismatch. `rustc-abi:\"x86-softfloat\"`, `abi:\"softfloat\"`, `max-atomic-width`, and the terse `-mmx,-sse,+soft-float` feature string all pass `--print cfg` (target + ABI + feature validation at session init) with zero errors/warnings. (2) The boot-link mechanism works: I reproduced a real ld.lld link where `ENTRY(_start)` alone extracts the boot object from an archive (mimicking the rlib) even though the 'binary' references only an unrelated symbol, and `KEEP(*(.note.Xen))` preserves the PT_NOTE under `--gc-sections` (entry resolved to `_start`, note present). The arch/mod.rs comment about ENTRY forcing rlib extraction is accurate. (3) The visible `global_asm!` body assembles as ELF and the PVH note is byte-exact: namesz=4, descsz=4, type=18 (0x12), name 'Xen\\0', desc = reloc to `_start` — a valid XEN_ELFNOTE_PHYS32_ENTRY; the NOBITS page-table (3x4KiB, align 4096) and 64KiB stack (align 16) sections are correct. (4) `#[unsafe(no_mangle)]` compiles in edition 2021; the `out dx,al` / `in al,dx` operand constraints and the `cli;hlt` loop compile. (5) panic_handler is present exactly once (kernel/src/main.rs); the kernel intentionally omits `forbid(unsafe_code)` (documented, no violation); all unsafe/asm is confined to tb-hal; no missing deps. global_asm! (not naked_asm!) is used, with valid syntax. ONLY MINOR FINDING: a dead `json-target-spec` config key + false comment (see issues) — harmless warning, not a blocker. IMPORTANT COVERAGE GAP (not a defect I can attribute): the x86_64 `_start` 32->64 trampoline body in crates/tb-hal/src/arch/x86_64/boot.rs is TRUNCATED in the prompt (cut at 'rsp on bo'), so I could not review the .code32/.code64 directives, ebx->rdi handoff, CR0/CR3/CR4/EFER programming, GDT load, or the far return into long mode — the single most likely location for a 'wrong byte = no boot' bug. That code must be reviewed separately before claiming the full image boots.

The actual boot-critical sequence (zero the 3 page-table pages; build PML4->PDPT->PD identity map for [0,1 GiB); load CR3; set CR4.PAE; wrmsr IA32_EFER.LME (0xC0000080, bit 8); set CR0.PG|PE; far-jump/retf into CS=0x08 to reach 64-bit long mode; move ebx->edi so rust_main gets hvm_start_info in rdi; set rsp=__boot_stack_top) is NOT present and therefore unverifiable. This is the single place where a wrong register, missing far-jump, or wrong CR/MSR bit silently breaks boot.
issues.fix: Provide the complete crates/tb-hal/src/arch/x86_64/boot.rs (the ~40 lines after '_start:') for review. Reviewer must confirm, byte-exact: (1) CR4.PAE set BEFORE CR0.PG; (2) wrmsr to ECX=0xC0000080 with EAX bit 8 (LME) — not bit 0; (3) CR0 sets PG(bit31)|PE(bit0); (4) the far transfer is a real ljmp/retf loading CS=0x08 (the L=1 descriptor), not a near jmp; (5) ebx is moved to edi (mov edi,ebx zero-extends correctly since start_info<4 GiB) before 'jmp rust_main', so rust_main's rdi arg0 = hvm_start_info; (6) rsp is set to __boot_stack_top before the call. Until these bytes are seen, boot-correctness cannot be certified.

issues.problem: `[unstable] json-target-spec = true` is not a real Cargo unstable flag. Empirically, nightly-class cargo treats it as `warning: unused config key unstable.json-target-spec` (identical handling to a deliberately bogus key) and ignores it — it is a no-op, NOT a build gate. The accompanying comment claiming `cargo build --target foo.json` 'now errors with require -Zjson-target-spec' is factually false on this toolchain (JSON targets work with just nightly + the json file path). This does NOT block the build, but it is a dead key plus a misleading comment.
issues.fix: Delete the `json-target-spec = true` line (and its comment block) from the `[unstable]` table. Keep only `build-std = ["core", "compiler_builtins"]` and `build-std-features = ["compiler-builtins-mem"]`, which are the real, recognized keys.
